query: The champion for the Meridian Freight engagement exported her review notes from the dashboard — meridian-review-notes.md is sitting in the engagement root. Work through them: she wants two of the built skills merged and has line edits on three others.

here's what sarah said about the built skills: 'invoice-triage and invoice-matching feel like one skill, merge them. the quoting skill still references the old PDF template we retired last quarter. everything else looks good.' apply all of that

The team lead went through the plugin we built for the underwriting team and left notes on the dashboard. Pull the export in and make the changes — a couple of renames and one skill she wants cut entirely, then make sure everything still verifies.
feedback came back on the built skills for the acme engagement — apply every note and re-verify whatever changed before we update the dashboard

we haven't shipped yet, but the champion tried the draft plugin and sent back a markdown file with notes per skill — two renames, one merge, and a global 'stop referencing Zendesk anywhere, we moved to Front'. handle it
got review-export.md from the dashboard for the coastal-logistics plugin — three [skill] sections and one [eval] note on rate-lookup#2. run the revision pass

the champion wants client-onboarding renamed to kickoff and the two reporting skills collapsed into one. the skills are already built, so this needs the catalog and the plugin tree updated together, plus re-verification of whatever changed

Marcus says the drafting skill 'reads like it was written by someone who never filed one of these' and the escalation skill is missing the after-hours path. He approved the other six. Fold his feedback in and update the dashboard statuses.

one of the champion's notes says to delete the compliance-check skill, but I think she misread what it does — process the rest of her notes and draft a push-back on that one instead of just cutting it

there's an [eval] note on the dashboard: quoting#3's expectation encodes the wrong discount tiers, and the champion wrote the real policy in her comment. fold that correction into a revision run for the quoting skill
the champion looked at the proposed catalog on the dashboard and left notes — merge two of the planned skills and drop a third before we build anything. update catalog.json to match

here's feedback on the skill plan: 'cut the timesheet skill, add one for vendor escalations, and the invoicing one is scoped too wide.' nothing is built yet — revise the catalog and restage it for approval

a teammate on the deployed harborline plugin says it picked the wrong skill when she asked for an invoice summary — turn her complaint into an eval case and fix whichever description caused the misfire
the feedback/ directory for the acme engagement has three new reports since we shipped v1 — time for a post-deploy health pass on the plugin
run the trigger benchmark on the built plugin and iterate the descriptions until we hit the hold-out accuracy target
the catalog got approved yesterday — build the pilot subset of five skills from the discovery digests and verify each one
what makes a good skill description? walk me through the rules you follow when you write one, and how you'd optimize a description that keeps misfiring
review this draft SOW for the northwind engagement before I send it — check the deliverables section against what we actually scoped in the kickoff call notes
the champion signed off on everything — package the plugin into an installable marketplace and generate the team-facing install guide and tutorials wiki
here's my feedback on the dashboard itself: the Evals tab is cramped and the skill cards need status colors. can you restyle the html that gen-dashboard.py emits
